prerequisites: A SendOwl account with the API enabled. The API is not enabled by default, so you must turn it on in your account settings before credentials can be generated.

An API key and secret generated from Settings > SendOwl API (https://dashboard.sendowl.com/settings/api_credentials). When creating the key, assign permissions (the Manager checkbox grants full read and write access).
A start date for the sync window, provided to the connector's start_date config field, used to bound how far back records are extracted.

authMethods.name: HTTP Basic Auth (API key and secret)
authMethods.summary: SendOwl authenticates every request with HTTP Basic Auth, passing your API key and secret. In this connector, supply the API key as the username config field and the API secret as the password secret field. Requests should be sent over HTTPS because the key and secret are otherwise transmitted in plaintext.

setupSteps.title: Enable the API on your SendOwl account
setupSteps.body: Log into SendOwl and open Settings, then click SendOwl API. The API is not enabled by default, so this step turns it on for your account.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key and secret
setupSteps.body: On the SendOwl API settings page (https://dashboard.sendowl.com/settings/api_credentials), click Add API key, assign the desired permissions (select the Manager checkbox for full read and write access), then save. SendOwl displays the resulting API key and secret. Treat these like a password and do not share them.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials to the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter your SendOwl API key as the username field and your API secret as the password field. These are sent to the SendOwl API (https://api.sendowl.com/api/v1) via HTTP Basic Auth over HTTPS.

setupSteps.title: Set the sync start date
setupSteps.body: Provide a value for the required start_date config field to define the earliest point from which records are extracted.

sources.title: SendOwl API - Introduction (authentication, base URL, Basic Auth)
Enabling and using the SendOwl API (create API key steps)
